differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis due to prior abdominal trauma
next_steps: Re-evaluate abdominal imaging (MR angiography or CT angiography) to assess portal venous patency and rule out mesenteric ischemia
Perform liver biopsy with specific histochemical stains for Schistosoma eggs and fibrosis patterns
Perform serum and stool studies for parasitic infection (e.g., Schistosoma serology, O&P)
Consult interventional radiology for possible portal vein recanalization or TIPS consideration if symptomatic portal hypertension persists
Assess cardiac function via echocardiography to rule out right-sided heart failure contributing to congestive hepatopathy
Monitor nutritional status and manage chronic abdominal pain with non-opioid strategies

differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal hypertensive enteropathy
next_steps: Perform CT angiography or MR angiography to evaluate mesenteric vasculature and exclude chronic mesenteric ischemia
Perform ultrasound-guided percutaneous liver biopsy with specific histochemical stains (e.g., Trichrome, iron, Schistosoma evaluation)
Genetic testing for HFE mutation to investigate elevated transferrin saturation
Serologic and stool studies for Schistosoma mansoni
Transthoracic echocardiogram to rule out underlying cardiac contributions to hepatic congestion
Nutritional assessment and optimization, considering the 20 kg weight loss
Multidisciplinary review involving interventional radiology and hepatology for potential portal hypertension management
